The '''65th British Academy Film Awards''', more commonly known as the [[BAFTAs]], were held on 12 February 2012 at the [[Royal Opera House]] in [[London]], honouring the best national and foreign films of [[2011 in film|2011]]. Presented by the [[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]], accolades are handed out for the best feature-length film and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at British cinemas in 2011.

The nominations were announced on 17 January 2012 by actor [[Daniel Radcliffe]] and actress [[Holliday Grainger]].<ref>{{cite web |title=British Academy Film Awards in 2012 – Longlist |url=http://static.bafta.org/files/longlist-1112-1256.pdf |website=bafta.org |publisher=British Academy of Film and Television Arts |access-date=25 January 2026 |ref=Longlist |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140910110107/http://static.bafta.org/files/longlist-1112-1256.pdf |archive-date=10 September 2014}}</ref><ref>[http://www.bafta.org/film/awards/nominees-winners-2012,2449,BA.html BAFTA Film Awards Nominations in 2012], bafta.org, 17 January 2012.</ref> ''[[The Artist (film)|The Artist]]'' won seven awards out of its twelve nominations, including Best Film, Best Director for [[Michel Hazanavicius]], and Best Actor for [[Jean Dujardin]]. [[Meryl Streep]] won Best Actress for ''[[The Iron Lady (film)|The Iron Lady]]''. [[Christopher Plummer]] won Best Supporting Actor for ''[[Beginners]]'' and [[Octavia Spencer]] won Best Supporting Actress for ''[[The Help (film)|The Help]]''. ''[[Tinker Tailor Soldier Spy (film)|Tinker Tailor Soldier Spy]]'', directed by [[Tomas Alfredson]], was voted Outstanding British Film of 2011.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.digitalspy.co.uk/movies/news/a364820/orange-bafta-film-awards-2012-winners-list-in-full.html|title=Orange BAFTA Film Awards 2012 winners list – in full|last=Reynolds|first=Simon|date=12 February 2012|work=[[Digital Spy]]|publisher=[[Hearst Magazines UK]]|access-date=12 February 2012}}</ref> Director [[Martin Scorsese]] was given the [[BAFTA Fellowship]] and [[John Hurt|Sir John Hurt]] garnered the [[BAFTA Outstanding British Contribution to Cinema Award]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.reuters.com/article/idUS48227207120120212|title='The Artist' Dominates at BAFTA Awards|last=Pond|first=Steve|date=12 February 2012|publisher=[[Reuters]]|access-date=12 February 2012}}</ref>

[[Stephen Fry]], who hosted from 2001 to 2006, returned to host the ceremony.<ref>[http://www.bafta.org/press/stephen-fry-returns-to-british-academy-film-awards,162,SNS.html Stephen Fry Returns to British Academy Film Awards], bafta.org.</ref>
